title: Microwave resonator flame ionization detector
abstract: A microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly (316) includes a microwave resonator (504) disposed proximate a flame (322) to evaluate an ion concentration in a flame effluent. A resonant frequency of the microwave resonator (504) is detected, and a reflection coefficient of the resonator (504) is used to determine an electric permittivity of a material in which the resonator (5040 is immersed. The electric permittivity depends on an ion concentration proximal to the resonator (504), and the ion concentration is related to the concentration of hydrocarbons present in the flame (322).
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly, comprising: a microwave resonator disposed proximate a flame to evaluate an ion concentration in a flame effluent; wherein a resonant frequency of the resonator is detected, and a reflection coefficient of the resonator is used to determine an electric permittivity of a material in which the resonator is immersed; and wherein the electric permittivity depends on an ion concentration proximal to the resonator, and wherein the ion concentration is related to the concentration of hydrocarbons present in the flame. 2. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 1, and further comprising: a burner assembly configured to receive a gas sample that includes the concentration of hydrocarbons from a gas delivery subsystem and generates the flame based on the gas sample. 3. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 2, wherein the resonator comprises a hairpin resonator coupled to the burner assembly as part of a probe assembly. 4. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 3, and further comprising: a meter coupled to the resonator configured to detect the reflection coefficient of the resonator as the flame effluent acts on the resonator and generate signals indicative of the reflection coefficient. 5. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 4, wherein the meter comprises a reflectometer or a network analyzer configured to sense the reflection coefficient of the resonator. 6. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 4, and further comprising: a controller coupled to the meter configured to receive the signals from the meter coupled to the resonator and, based on the signals, determine the electric permittivity of the material in which the resonator is immersed. 7. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 6, wherein the controller is further configured to determine the concentration of hydrocarbons within the gas sample based on the electric permittivity of the material in which the resonator is immersed. 8. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 1, wherein the reflection coefficient comprises Sn parameter information. 9. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 2, wherein the burner assembly comprises: an envelope configured to house the flame; an end cap configured to couple to both the envelope and the resonator; and a burner cell coupled to the envelope configured to receive the gas sample from the gas delivery subsystem and generate the flame. 10. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 9, wherein the burner cell comprises: a burner tip configured to orient the flame within the envelope; a plug coupled to the gas delivery subsystem configured to receive the gas sample from the gas delivery subsystem; a port coupled to the gas delivery subsystem configured to receive a different gas sample from the gas delivery subsystem; and an ignitor configured to receive electrical power from a power supply and generate the flame. 11. The microwave resonator flame ionization detector assembly of claim 4, wherein the probe assembly comprises: a feed line coupled to the resonator using a direct coupling between the feed line and the resonator; and an adapter coupled to the feed line and the meter. 12. A method of sensing hydrocarbons in a gas sample, the method comprising: subjecting a gas sample to a flame to generate ions; providing a microwave resonator proximate the ions; detecting a characteristic of the resonator while the resonator is subjected to the ions; and detecting a concentration of hydrocarbons in the gas sample based on the detected characteristic of the resonator. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein subjecting the gas sample to the flame comprises: generating control signals for a gas delivery subsystem to provide the gas sample to a burner assembly configured to generate the flame; generating control signals for a vacuum subsystem to reduce a pressure within the burner assembly; and generating control signals for a power supply coupled to the burner assembly to electrically bias the burner assembly to maintain an ion-electron disassociation within the burner assembly. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein detecting the characteristic of the resonator comprises: receiving signals from a meter coupled to the resonator indicative of the characteristic of the resonator, wherein the characteristic corresponds to a scattering parameter of the resonator; and determining the scattering parameter of the resonator based on the received signals. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein detecting the concentration of hydrocarbons comprises: determining a resonant frequency and a quality factor of the resonator based on the scattering parameter of the resonator; determining an electric permittivity of an area proximate the resonator based on the determined resonant frequency of the resonator; and determining the ion concentration within the gas sample based on the determined electric permittivity of the area proximate the resonator. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the meter comprises a reflectometer or a network analyzer configured to measure the scattering parameter of the resonator. 17. A gas chromatograph, comprising: a microwave resonator flame ionization detector; and a network analyzer or reflectometer coupled to the microwave resonator flame ionization detector to evaluate characteristics of the microwave resonator flame ionization detector. 18. The gas chromatograph of claim 17, wherein the microwave resonator flame ionization detector comprises: a burner assembly configured to receive a gas sample from a gas delivery subsystem and generate a flame comprising the gas sample; and 

 a probe assembly coupled to the burner assembly configured to receive a flame effluent from the flame, the probe assembly comprising a resonator configured to receive the flame effluent. 19. The gas chromatograph of claim 18, further comprising: a controller coupled to the network analyzer or reflectometer configured to receive signals from the network analyzer or reflectometer indicative of a scattering parameter of the resonator as the resonator receives the flame effluent. 20. The gas chromatograph of claim 19, wherein the controller is further configured to determine an electric permittivity of an area proximate the resonator based on the received signals from the network analyzer or reflectometer, and, based on the electric permittivity, a hydrocarbon ion concentration within the gas sample.
